behaviors.description: A static prompt-template lint check rejects phase-specific instruction leakage copied from SKILL.md.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is pure lint logic over template text (MOCK-MISS: no cross-module handoff required). No real OS/network/subprocess or model output is needed. Rejected Integration/System/Agentic because the behavior text is explicitly a static lint check, not harness assembly, live infra, or LLM judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test the lint rule with template fixtures containing verbatim or near-verbatim phase instructions from SKILL.md; assert rejection with precise diagnostics and allow non-leaking templates.

behaviors.description: The fully assembled prompt produced by the real test harness (after interpolation and fixtures) still contains no phase-instruction leakage.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears when multiple local modules compose the final prompt (template + interpolation + fixtures), so Unit-only can miss it (MOCK-MISS yes). No real external API/subprocess required (REAL-INFRA no), and correctness does not depend on model output (LLM-DEP no). Rejected System/Agentic due to behavior text focusing on assembled prompt content before model execution.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real prompt assembly pipeline end-to-end in-process with realistic fixtures; assert final assembled prompt text has no phase-instruction leakage signatures.

behaviors.description: The test invocation loads skill context from plugin/skill files and validates behavior from that context rather than from hard-coded prompt rules.
behaviors.justification: This is module wiring plus filesystem-backed skill loading and invocation behavior (MOCK-MISS yes). Local filesystem use fits Integration. Rejected System because no real HTTP/subprocess/external round-trip is required by the text. Rejected Agentic because this behavior can be validated as source-of-truth/orchestration (context loaded from files vs hard-coded rules) without requiring model-quality judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Execute test invocation through real loader/resolver against plugin/skill files, with controls proving behavior changes track file-provided context and not embedded prompt constants.

behaviors.description: The harness can run the same user-style prompt against an intentionally degraded or missing SKILL.md variant without changing test prompt text.
behaviors.justification: Requires harness orchestration across prompt input, variant selection, and skill-file loading (MOCK-MISS yes). Local file variants are enough; no real external infra needed. Rejected System due to no required subprocess/network boundary. Rejected Agentic because this behavior only checks harness capability to run unchanged prompt across variants, not output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run the harness twice (baseline and degraded/missing SKILL.md variant) with identical user-style prompt text; assert only skill-context source changes and execution still proceeds.

behaviors.description: When the relevant SKILL.md section is removed or degraded, the behavioral test fails under the same user-style prompt.
behaviors.justification: Pass/fail depends on model behavior changing due to skill-context degradation (LLM-DEP yes; deterministic stubs can hide this). Not a multi-agent pipeline requirement, so Workflow not required. Rejected Integration because behavior text demands behavioral failure from degraded SKILL.md under same prompt, which is exactly model-decision sensitivity.
behaviors.test_description: Run live model tests with identical user-style prompt across baseline vs degraded SKILL.md; assert degraded variant crosses failure threshold on behavioral criteria while baseline passes.

behaviors.description: Raw model output is captured to a diagnostic artifact before any JSON parsing or extraction logic runs.
behaviors.justification: This is sequencing across model-call wrapper, artifact writer, and parser/extractor modules (MOCK-MISS yes). Can be verified with controlled model output and local artifact storage. Rejected System because no real external infra is required beyond local in-process/file wiring. Rejected Agentic because ordering assertion is independent of model-output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Exercise the real harness pipeline with a controlled response source; assert artifact write occurs with unmodified raw output before parser/extractor invocation (e.g., via timestamps/hooks/order markers).

behaviors.description: Assertions enforce structural behavior constraints (for example schema paths and role-specific field placement), not only keyword presence checks.
behaviors.justification: Needs assertion framework wired into behavioral test execution, not just isolated predicate code (MOCK-MISS yes). No mandatory real external infra. Rejected Agentic because this behavior is about assertion mechanism coverage (structural vs keyword) and can be validated with crafted outputs independent of model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Run behavioral assertion stack on fixtures containing keyword matches but structural violations; assert failures occur on schema/field-placement rules and passes require correct structure.

behaviors.description: Under live model execution, structural assertions catch regressions that a keyword-only check would miss.
behaviors.justification: Explicitly requires live model execution and regression detection dependent on real model outputs (LLM-DEP yes). A stubbed model could mask the miss. Rejected Workflow because behavior describes a single live execution/check outcome, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Execute live model runs on prompts known to produce occasional keyword-pass/structure-fail outputs; compare assertion modes and verify structural checks fail cases that keyword-only logic would incorrectly pass.
